{{nihongo|'''Kawamata Shrine'''|河俣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=河俣神社 |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
社号標と鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}雲梯町689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=鴨八重{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} }} '''河俣神社'''（かわまたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|鴨八重事代主神}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 『{{jalink|出雲国造神賀詞|Q11395857}}』に{{jalink|皇室|Q909452}}を守護する神として「事代主命の御魂を宇奈提の神奈備に坐せ」とあり、この「宇奈提（うなせ）」は「雲梯（うなて）」のことであるとして、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載される式内大社「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|高市郡|Q1117901}} 高市御縣坐鴨事代主神社」に比定されている（延喜式神名帳には「川俣神社三座」という社名が見えるが、これは近くの{{jalink|木葉神社}}に比定されている）。 『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』によれば、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}の際、高市郡大領高市縣主許梅に「高市社に居る事代主神」と「{{jalink|牟佐坐神社|Q38278497|身狭社}}に居る{{jalink|天活玉命|Q97184246|生霊神}}」が{{jalink|神懸り}}し、「{{jalink|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇の陵}}に、馬及び種々の兵器を奉れ」と言い、そうすれば大海人皇子（後の{{jalink|天武天皇|Q335181}}）を守護すると{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}した。大海人皇子の即位後、この霊験により、高市御縣坐鴨事代主神に、史上初となる{{jalink|神位}}が授けられている。{{jalink|万葉集|Q834321}}にも当社のことを詠んだ歌がある。 {{jalink|住吉大社|Q705949}}（{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|住吉区|Q985702}}）には{{jalink|畝火山口神社|Q11578590}}境内における埴取の神事があるが、その祭祀を行う者は当社で装束を整えることが恒例となっており、そのことから「装束の宮」と俗称される。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]]

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kashihara, Nara|Q496319}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{jalink|imperial house|Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{jalink|Konoha Shrine}}). According to the ''{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}'', during the {{jalink|Jinshin War|Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{jalink|Musa-jinja|Q38278497|Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{jalink|divine rank}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{jalink|Man'yōshū|Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949}} ({{jalink|Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702}}, {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{jalink|Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill||lt=Konoha Shrine|UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill||lt=divine rank|UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
