'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわまたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)}}[[Category:qq]]

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かわまたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|WD=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|WD=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|WD=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|qid=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|qid=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|qid=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|qid=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|qid=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|qid=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|qid=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|qid=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|qid=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

'奈良県橿原市雲梯町689', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kawamata-jinja}} [[JA:河俣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553360}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kawamata Shrine |画像=[[File:Kawamata Shrine Kashihara.jpg|270px]]
Shrine name marker and torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Unate-chō 689 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 7.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 27.9 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nara |祭神=Kamo-yahae {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|qid=Q2921|1=October 10}} }} '''Kawamata Shrine''' (かわまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara, Nara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kamo-yahae Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. === History === In the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto|Q11395857|qid=Q11395857|1=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto}}'', it says, as a deity protecting the {{ill|lt=imperial house|Q909452|qid=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, "Seat the august spirit of Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto in the divine provision of Unase." This "Unase" is identified with "Unate," and the shrine is thus identified with the Shikinai Taisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamo Kotoshironushi Shrine seated at Takachi Mikata, Takaichi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}" (although the ''Jinmyōchō'' also contains the name "three seats of Kawamata Shrine," this is identified with nearby {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konoha Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). According to the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|qid=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the district chief Takachi Agatanushi Kome was possessed by "Kotoshironushi-no-Kami who dwells at Takachi Shrine" and "Ikumusubi-no-Kami (Ame-ikutama-no-Mikoto) who dwells at {{ill|lt=Musa-jinja|Q38278497|qid=Q38278497|lt=Musa Shrine|1=Musanimasu Shrine}}." They delivered an oracle saying, "Offer horses and various weapons to the mausoleum of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu#Mausoleum and spirit shrine|Q200188|qid=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarebiko no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}," and promised to protect Prince Ōama (later {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). After Prince Ōama's enthronement, because of this miracle, the first historical conferment of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to Kamo Kotoshironushi-no-Kami seated at Takachi Mikata. The {{ill|lt=Man'yōshū|Q834321|qid=Q834321|1=Man'yōshū}} also contains a poem about this shrine. At {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi-ku|Q985702|qid=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}), there is the clay-taking ritual in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Hatakayama-guchi Shrine|Q11578590|qid=Q11578590|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}. The person who performs that rite customarily puts on ritual garments at this shrine, and for that reason it is popularly called "the shrine of vestments." === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawamata Shrine (Q11553360)]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]
